REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANYS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHIFT ITS BURDEN AND SHOW THAT IT IS ENTITLED AS A MATTER OF LAW TO A JUDGMENT ON A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT AS A SUBROGEE. SEE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES 4-8. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B); NO "SPECIFIC CAUSE OF ACTION, AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, CLAIMS FOR DAMAGES, OR ISSUES OF DUTY" ARE STATED IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Plaintiff National Title Insurance Companys Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Christopher Thomas, 925.817.3718 of Fidelity National Law Group of Plaintiff Fidelity National Title Insurance Company.
Alec Harrington, 415.781.4000 of Stanaland & Associates for Defendant Valerie Tashjian.
Kevin Sullivan, 415.860.2170 for Defendant Ralph Tashjian.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff National Title Insurance Companys Motion For Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that it is entitled as a matter of law to a judgment on a cause of action for breach of implied covenant as a subrogee. See Points and Authorities 4-8. The Court further notes that moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b); no "specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty" are stated in the separate statement. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Application For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment against defendants Ralph and Valerie Tashjian is DENIED. Construing the statutory language strictly as required by case law (Pacific Decision Sciences Corp. v. Superior Court (2004) 121 Cal. App. 4th 1100, 1106), Fidelity's claim for breach of implied warranty of title is "based on the sale ' of property ' where the property sold 'was used by the defendant primarily for personal, family, or household purposes"(CCP 483.010(c)) and thus is not a claim for which an attachment may be issued. (Id.). But for the Tashjians' sale of their home to Fidelity's insured, Fidelity would have no claim. The Tashjians' obligations per the implied warranty of title doctrine arose from the sale of their home and their decision to secure a loan by a deed of trust against their home. The fact that the loan was a "business loan" does not convert the sale transaction which triggered the warranty obligation from a sale of a residence to a business transaction.

Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported (L&M302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2017 Continued To Feb-06-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VALERIE TASHJIAN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR RESTITUTION BASED ON (1) UNJUST ENRICHMENT AND (2) EQUITABLE INDEMNITY.

Defendant Valerie Tashjian's demurrer to the first and second causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Fidelity National Title Insurance Company is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action. The complaint does not necessarily show that either of Fidelity's claims are time barred. "A cause of action accrues upon the occurrence of the last element essential to the cause of action." (Howard Jarvis Taxpayers Ass'n v. City of La Habra (2001) 25 Cal.4th 809, 815.) Liberally construed, the complaint alleges that Fidelity's damages, an element of both claims, didn't accrue until July 3, 2014 when Fidelity issued a check to Mr. Quinn. Nonetheless, both claims are deficiently pled. Both claims are based on the allegation that the Tashjians failed to disclose their loan obligations to Mr. Quinn, yet nowhere in the complaint is there any allegation supporting a duty of disclose by the Tashjians. Fidelity is given leave to amend to allege ultimate facts supporting a duty to disclose and/or any viable claim against the Tashjians that is not based on a duty to disclose.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185, email:ADelgado@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)
